The turban style (large wrapped Sikh pagri with visible red underlay), full beard, and red bindi are characteristic of Sikh religious/cultural identity from later periods. Akbar lived 1542-1605 and Sikhism was just emerging; he would have worn Mughal court dress with jama and Mughal-style turban. The jewelry and rich fabrics show quality execution, but this is a fundamental subject/period mismatch. Image shows excellent technical quality with clear face and good composition. However, it depicts a Mughal-era or later period figure (likely 16th-18th century) rather than Early Medieval Chalukyan period (600-800 CE). The elaborate turban style with tail, long braided hair, styled mustache, and bindi are characteristic of much later periods. Chalukyan period would show simpler head coverings, different jewelry styles, and different textile patterns based on temple reliefs. The clothing appears too refined/tailored for 600-800 CE.
